svg.title: Hamster mini-happy v1 (scripted)
svg.desc: Mini-mode happy (attention / task-complete celebration) for clawd-on-desk / MiniCPM-Desk-Pet, right-edge variant (host mirrors for left edge). Storyboard confirmed by 鹿鹿 2026-06-12 and refreshed 2026-06-15 for the "too stiff" board note: the empty task-list clipboard is already present from the first frame and starts with an obvious short damped wobble, then gently floats/rotates in screen coordinates (warm-brown board + cream paper + abstract dash rows, all character palette) -> three checkboxes get ticked in quick succession (stroke-draw, staggered 0.3s, each landing gives the board a tiny damped nudge plus a small rotational click) -> after all three ticks land, the board does a deliberately obvious left-right sway before settling, even if the host crop trims the wider motion -> the hamster snaps its head toward the board (~150ms, step-fed look target into the verbatim mini-idle engine; never feed a continuous trajectory, the 150ms segment interpolator would damp it) -> once all three land, the list "settles": rows and small ticks fade out and one big soft-green check (#7fb069, draw + pop) appears on the paper -> after the green check finishes, the board gains a subtle scale-breath and slow rotational drift so the hold no longer freezes -> seeing it, the face blooms: happy open-mouth smile (verbatim task-complete-loop-v1 mask rig: opening clip > door clip > shade/cavity/tongue fills, sideY 200 bottomY 219.5 halfW 6 doorInset 8.5 doorRound 13) + smile brows (0,-3) + white sunglass star + two golden four-point stars + 8 deterministic confetti chips (one-shot, no randomness) -> turns back smiling -> hold loop: mouth pulse, tuft float, tie micro-sway via wrapper (verbatim base-pose tie transforms untouched), board bobbing/breathing/rotating, star twinkle. Pose contract: paws stay clipped to the screen edge and the body never translates (mini no-jump rule); emotion is told through the prop and the face only. Engine drives breathing/look layers itself; happy writes only screen-coordinate prop layers, per-tuft/brow/mouth nodes and the happy-tie-sway wrapper, so the two never fight. Total entrance ≈2.6s then holds forever; host auto-returns after ~4s. Self-driven, no pointer feed needed; svg root keeps pointer-events:none so the engine hover fallback can never steal the gaze in embedding pages. ?loop=1 replays the full theater (showcase/preview). Exposes window.__happyDebug.replay() for demos. Pure paths + soft-lift filter, no image/gradient/fetch.
